title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 July 2024.

items.title: The face of Supermassive Games' The **Dark Pictures Anthology**, **Tony Pankhurst** (The Curator), dies aged 67
bullets: Although Pankhurst died in May 2024, Supermassive only today (July 29) shared the news across its social media channels.

Unlike some of the other notable faces and names attached to The Dark Pictures Anthology since the series began in 2019, in this instance, Pankhurst provided the facial likeness of The Curator, whilst Pip Torrens provided the voice and motion capture.

items.title: Removing **skill-based matchmaking** leads to a drop in players, Activision study suggests
bullets: The publisher held an experiment in Modern Warfare 3 earlier this year

For its experiment, Activision took the North American player base for Modern Warfare 3. For half the players it ran a ‘Deprioritise Skill Test’, which “loosened the constraints” on skill-based matchmaking, meaning less emphasis was placed on a player’s skills when looking for opponents

According to Activision’s findings, most of the players in the Deprioritise Skill Test were less likely to come back to the game. While the top 10% highest skilled players returned to the game in increased numbers within two weeks (because they were winning more), when it came to the other 90%, fewer returned to the game than normal.

items.title: **Grasshopper Manufacture** to host second annual showcase later this week
**Castle Crashers** is getting new DLC 16 years after it first released
bullets: 12 years after it first arrived on Steam, and 16 years after it debuted on Xbox Live Arcade, Castle Crashers is getting new DLC.
Although Castle Crashers debuted on Xbox and is still available on Sony consoles and Nintendo Switch, the DLC is only coming to Steam

items.title: **Double Fine** drops a 90-minute update to its 20+ hour series documenting the development of Psychonauts 2
bullets: The episode chronicles the studio "reflecting on the launch of Psychonauts 2 and the PsychOdyssey documentary series while looking to the future and a return to smaller projects".

items.title: **Mortal Kombat 1: Khaos Reigns** DLC announced; Animalities, T-1000, Conan, and Scream's Ghostface all on the way
**Embracer's Plaion** will publish Uncharted director’s Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra
bullets: The publisher (formerly known as Koch Media) has entered into a “worldwide publishing partnership” with the game’s developer, Skydance Games.

items.title: **Hasbro** CEO: "We're going all in on becoming a digital play company"
bullets: "Our goal is to be shipping one to two new games per year starting as early as late 2025, potentially early 2026," Chris Cocks says

items.title: **Electric Square**'s Lively Studio faces small number of layoffs
bullets: "Limited to the sister studio" - Electric Square unaffected for now

items.title: **Capcom** Q1 FY25 Results
bullets: For the three months ended June 30, 2024:
Net sales: ¥29.6 billion ($192 million), down 32.5% year-on-year
Digital Contents net sales: ¥21.4 billion ($138 million), down 43.5% year-on-year
Net income: ¥9.97 billion ($64.7 million), down 45.1% year-on-year
Arcade Operations up 18%, and Amusement Equipments up 147%
